Middle East Fuel Oil Exports Set to Hit Four-Month High
headlines.summary: Fuel oil exports from the Middle East are expected to rebound to a four-month high this month as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz picks up and key fuel oil producers Iraq, Saudi Arabia, and Oman have moved to increase shipments from ports outside the Persian Gulf. headlines.title: Russia Faces Growing Fuel Crunch as Ukrainian Strikes Knock Out Refineries
headlines.summary: Russia is facing its worst nationwide fuel shortages in years, with at least 17 regions imposing mandatory restrictions on gasoline and diesel sales, and dozens of others reporting shortages or restrictions by private fuel companies. headlines.title: ADNOC Cuts Murban Crude Price to $101.48 as Hormuz Tensions Ease
headlines.summary: ADNOC, the national oil company of Abu Dhabi, has cut the official selling price for July for its flagship Murban crude to $101.48 per barrel, down from $104.44 a barrel for June, amid weakening international and Middle Eastern benchmark oil prices. headlines.title: China to Increase Fuel Export Allowances for July
headlines.summary: China is raising the volume of fuels it is allowing state refiners to export, in a move that would alleviate concerns about tight refined petroleum supplies in Asia. headlines.title: $70 Oil Could Put India Back on Track for 7% Economic Growth
headlines.summary: India’s economy could return to the trajectory to grow by 7% and even more in the fiscal year through March 2027 if oil prices remain close to the current $70 per barrel, according to a senior official at India’s central bank. headlines.title: Iraq Could Quit OPEC in Bid to Pump More Oil
